purpose: Machine-readable workflow, approval, Work Hub, planned-work, notification, evidence, and object-timeline lifecycle contract for the platform-maturity ultragoal. This gate proves source-object centered workflow operations are wired through backend contracts, frontend actions, browser matrices, and regression tests before asset, collaboration, ERP, and live-rollout lanes depend on them.
nonClaimPolicy: G005 may prove local contract, browser matrix, and regression coverage. Production Argo rollout, live screenshot/trace closure, and live DB/API/browser parity remain G009; any route audit screenshotTraceEvidence that is still Pending must be treated as a rollout blocker, not as live closure.

routePaths.requiredStory: first-screen enterprise action inbox shows today/week tasks, approvals, messages, tickets, urgent badges, terminal removal, and group/org scope controls without maintenance-only framing
manager approval queue requires comment or rejection memo, shows source object, evidence images, approval line actors, history, and removes terminal decisions
authorized manager builds, simulates, publishes, pauses, rolls back, and clones workflows with approval/payment lines, passkey step-up, audit, and connector allowlists
work-order detail supports dispatch control, priority, schedule change, mechanic assignment, outsource work, evidence image view, approval line comments, and immutable object timeline
received work requests create source objects that later planned work, dispatch, approval, notifications, evidence, and audit can reference
mechanic planned work starts from received work order, requests manager review, records review memo, and reaches final confirmation without bypassing approval lifecycle

dependencyRoutes.requiredStory: dispatch board actions must link back to work order detail and workflow/audit lifecycle before G006 claims maturity
support ticket action inbox removal and object-linked comments use workflow lifecycle primitives before G008 CX claims maturity
finance and procurement requests cannot claim maturity until purchase/payment lines are routed through the approval workflow lifecycle

backendContracts.contract: Workflow Studio is not cosmetic configuration: it exposes versioned simulate/publish lifecycle, internal connectors, required approval and payment lines, passkey step-up, audit, and request instrumentation.
Workflow definitions, versions, and events are RLS-scoped and append-only where needed; approval/payment lines and notification rules are persisted for review and rollback history.
requiredSnippets: GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON workflow_definitions

backendContracts.contract: Approval decisions are signing-equivalent events and must persist reviewer comment or rejection memo text with the approval step.
Approval feeds are server-owned and scoped; terminal work orders are removed, and each item deep-links to its source object or planned-work target.
Work-order approval and rejection require human rationale, persist decision_comment, create audit events, and daily plans keep requested/draft plans visible for review and confirmation.
Domain transitions enforce ordered approval line completion and verified completion evidence before final completion can be reached.
Work order and daily plan tables carry explicit approval and status history lifecycles that UI queues must reflect.
Evidence images and files are first-class work-order objects with WORM verification and an admin queue for unverified artifacts.
Evidence upload processing has explicit PROCESSING, READY, and FAILED lifecycle states so approval views can distinguish viewable images from pending artifacts.
Terminal work orders freeze completion evidence and notification leases prevent duplicate or stale alert state.

frontendContracts.contract: Workflow Studio exposes real authoring lifecycle operations and blocks publish until required approval/payment lines and passkey step-up are present.
Work Hub is a group-aware enterprise action inbox that combines server approval, daily plan, messenger, support, urgency, and partial-failure states without client-side security assumptions.
Approvals page consumes the federated server feed and routes approve/reject commands through comment-bearing actions.
Approval queue requires approval comment or rejection memo, lazily renders detail/evidence, and filters to actionable review statuses.
Planned-work authoring starts from received work orders, requests review, records review decision, and supports final confirmation lifecycle.
Work order detail hosts dispatch/edit operations so dispatch control lives at the source object rather than on a disconnected board.
Object detail renders evidence, approval line comments, and status history so managers can review what happened before approving or rejecting.
Support ticket lifecycle helpers keep resolved and closed tickets out of action inboxes while preserving reopen paths and SLA status.

safetyAssertions: Workflow publish, pause, rollback, clone, approval decisions, payment-line changes, and terminal status changes are signing-equivalent or sensitive actions and require passkey or equivalent step-up where implemented.
Workflow Builder must prove simulate before publish and reject missing approval or payment lines for templates that require them.
Approval and rejection decisions require visible human comment or memo and store the actor, decision, and rationale for audit and timeline display.
Approval queues and Work Hub queues remove terminal, closed, archived, cancelled, approved, or rejected objects from actionable counts and badges.
Evidence images/files uploaded by mechanics remain viewable in manager approval context and cannot be attached after terminal completion.
Planned work must originate from a received work order or explicit source object so future analytics can use historical request, equipment, site, and approval context.
Urgent priority, notifications, badges, messenger/mail/work comments, and support tickets are linked to the source object instead of floating as disconnected widgets.
Work-order detail is the canonical source-object control surface for dispatch edits, mechanic assignment, priority, schedule change, evidence, approval, and history.
Group admins see consolidated queues and can scope into subsidiaries only when workflow policy grants access; wrong-org approvals or edits are denied.
Live screenshot/trace/Argo closure remains G009 unless captured by production browser evidence.
